title: 3.6x increased risk of sexual dysfunction when taking SSRI Antidepressants
summary: 3.6x increased risk of sexual dysfunction when taking SSRI Antidepressants.
detail: Selective Seratonin Reuptake Inhibitors (citalopram (Celexa), escitalopram (Lexapro), fluoxetine (Prozac), paroxetine (Paxil), or sertraline (Zoloft)) cause worse sexual dysfunction (a common side-effect) in people with this SNP. Note that research has shown Saffron is effective at reversing the sexual dysfunction from SSRI anti-depressants.
whyItMatters: SNPedia has a genotype-specific page for this result, which can add consumer-facing context alongside primary evidence sources.
